summary:
The hypothalamus exerts an inhibitory influence over certain pituitary hormones such as prolactin and growth hormone; this regulation involves specific signaling pathways managed at the level of the brain-gland interface.
The hypothalamus regulates multiple pituitary hormones including prolactin and growth hormone with inhibitory effects, highlighting its critical role in hormonal homeostasis within the body. \boxed{\text{D}} To ensure clarity while addressing only essential information for answering about regulatory roles between glands and the central nervous system, focusing directly on options provided would refine our response further if needed but remains accurate based solely upon presented content regarding physiological interactions involving those particular endocrine functions described here.
